author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of David himself, before he was anointed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ’s young soldier speaketh, on his coming to the faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is my light, and my salvation: whom shall I fear?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord will give me both knowledge of Himself, and salvation: who shall take me from Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is the Protector of my life: of whom shall I be afraid?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord will repel all the assaults and snares of mine enemy: of no man shall I be afraid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whilst the guilty approach unto me to eat up my flesh” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whilst the guilty come near to recognise and insult me, that they may exalt themselves above me in my change for the better; that with their reviling tooth they may consume not me, but rather my fleshly desires.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine enemies who trouble me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not they only who trouble me, blaming me with a friendly intent, and wishing to recall me from my purpose, but mine enemies also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They became weak, and fell.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whilst then they do this with the desire of defending their own opinion, they became weak to believe better things, and began to hate the word of salvation, whereby I do what displeases them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If camps stand together against me, my heart will not fear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if the multitude of gain-sayers conspire to stand together against me, my heart will not fear, so as to go over to their side.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If war rise up against me, in this will I trust” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the persecution of this world arise against me, in this petition, which I am pondering, will I place my hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “One have I asked of the Lord, this will I require.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For one petition have I asked the Lord, this will I require.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That as long as I am in this life, no adversities may exclude me from the number of them who hold the unity and the truth of the Lord’s faith throughout the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That I may contemplate the delight of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With this end, namely, that persevering in the faith, the delightsome vision may appear to me, which I may contemplate face to face.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I shall be protected, His temple.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And death being swallowed up in victory, I shall be clothed with immortality, being made His temple.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He hath hidden me in His tabernacle in the day of my evils” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He hath hidden me in the dispensation of His Incarnate Word in the time of temptations, to which my mortal life is exposed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath protected me in the secret place of His tabernacle.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath protected me, with the heart believing unto righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “On a rock hath He exalted me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that what I believed might be made manifest for salvation, He hath made my confession to be conspicuous in His own strength.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And now, lo!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath exalted mine head above mine enemies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doth He reserve for me at the last, when even now the body is dead because of sin, lo!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I feel that my mind serves the law of God, and is not led captive under the rebellious law of sin?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have gone about, and have sacrificed in His tabernacle the sacrifice of rejoicing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have considered the circuit of the world, believing on Christ; and in that for us God was humbled in time, I have praised Him with rejoicing: for with such sacrifice He is well pleased.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will sing and give praises to the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In heart and in deed I will be glad in the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear my voice, O Lord, wherewith I have cried unto Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear, Lord, my interior voice, which with a strong intention I have addressed to Thy ears.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Have mercy upon me, and hear me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have mercy upon me, and hear me therein.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My heart hath said to Thee, I have sought Thy countenance” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I have not exhibited myself to men; but in secret, where Thou alone hearest, my heart hath said to Thee; I have not sought from Thee aught without Thee as a reward, but Thy countenance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy countenance, O Lord, will I seek.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In thus search will I perseveringly persist: for not aught that is common, but Thy countenance, O Lord, will I seek, that I may love Thee freely, since nothing more precious do I find.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn not away Thy face from me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9): that I may find what I seek.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn not aside in anger from Thy servant:” lest, while seeking Thee, I fall in with somewhat else.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is more grievous than this punishment to one who loveth and seeketh the truth of Thy countenance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be Thou my Helper.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How shall I find it, if Thou help me not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Leave me not, neither despise me, O God my Saviour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Scorn not that a mortal dares to seek the Eternal; for Thou, God, dost heal the wound of my sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For my father and my mother have left me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the kingdom of this world and the city of this world, of which I was born in time and mortality, have left me seeking Thee, and despising what they promised, since they could not give what I seek.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But the Lord took me up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Lord, who can give me Himself, took me up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Appoint me a law, O Lord, in Thy way” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For me then who am setting out toward Thee, and commenting so great a profession, of arriving at wisdom, from fear, appoint, O Lord, a law in Thy way, lest in my wandering Thy rule abandon me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And direct me in the right path because of mine enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And direct me in the right way of its straits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is not enough to begin, since enemies cease not until the end is attained.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver me not up unto the souls of them that trouble me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Suffer not them that trouble me to be satiated with my evils.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For unrighteous witnesses have risen up against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there have risen up against me they that speak falsely of me, to remove and call me back from Thee, as if I seek glory of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And iniquity hath lied unto itself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore iniquity hath been pleased with its own lie.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For me it hath not moved, to whom because of this there hath been promised a greater reward in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I believe to see the good things of the Lord in the land of the living” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And since my Lord hath first suffered these things, if I too despise the tongues of the dying (“for the mouth that lieth slayeth the soul”), I believe to see the good things of the Lord in the land of the living, where there is no place for falsity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Wait on the Lord, quit thyself like a man: and let thy heart be strong, yea wait on the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when shall this be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is arduous for a mortal, it is slow to a lover: but listen to the voice, that deceiveth not, of him that saith, “Wait on the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Endure the burning of the reins manfully, and the burning of the heart stoutly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not that what thou dost not as yet receive is denied thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That thou faint not in despair, see how it is said, “Wait on the Lord.”
